assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: In version-impact.md, the Version Impact table shows: 2.1.0 (h2 0.4.5) = YES, 2.1.1 (h2 0.4.5) = YES, 2.2.0 (h2 0.4.8) = NO, 2.2.1 (h2 0.4.8) = NO, 2.2.2 = NO (retag), 2.2.3 (h2 0.4.9) = NO, 2.2.4 (h2 0.4.9) = NO. The Stream Impact Summary confirms '2.1.x YES' and '2.2.x NO'. Evidence is based on lock file analysis of h2 versions at pinned build tags.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: In affects-versions.md, the Proposed (lock file evidence) row shows 'RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1' only. The correction details explicitly state: 'RHTPA 2.2.0: REMOVE -- h2 version 0.4.8 is the fixed version (>= 0.4.8), not affected'. The proposed Jira update sets versions to only RHTPA 2.1.0 and RHTPA 2.1.1.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 7 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, the heading states 'Case A -- Affected (2.1.x stream only)'. Two tasks are created both targeting 2.1.x: Task 1 targets 'release/0.3.z' (2.1.x upstream branch), Task 2 targets 'rhtpa-release.0.3.z'. A dedicated section 'Why No 2.2.x Remediation' explains '2.2.x stream ships h2 >= 0.4.8 in all versions... No remediation is needed for the 2.2.x stream.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: In data-extraction.md, Stream Scope is listed as 'Unscoped (no stream suffix in summary -- analyze all streams)'. The Stream Scope Resolution section states: 'The issue summary... contains no stream suffix in brackets. This issue is therefore unscoped and covers all configured version streams (2.1.x and 2.2.x). The version impact analysis must check all versions across all streams.' The version-impact.md table confirms all versions from both streams (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4) were checked.

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 7 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: In remediation.md, a dedicated section 'Why No Cross-Stream Impact Notice' states: 'TC-8004 is unscoped -- it has no stream suffix in the summary. Cross-stream impact notices (Case B) are only generated for scoped issues when streams outside the issue's scope are also affected. Since an unscoped issue already covers all streams, there are no "other streams outside scope" to notify about.'
